weaknesses.text: NEVER invent values. But DO infer quantity/unit when clearly present inside product names.
weaknesses.mechanism: Explicit permission to infer qty/unit from pack notation (*2, *6, 33cl*24) — directly causes Aceto/Rulo/POMODOR/Ginger Beer errors

weaknesses.text: Coca-Cola 33cl Pack 24 → qty 24; BAD: 33cl GOOD: 24 un
weaknesses.mechanism: Teaches bottle-count extraction but no rule for when pack spec is metadata vs purchased qty (CX 2.5KG*6 case qty=2 not 6)

weaknesses.text: Extract ALL invoice line items visible in the table.
weaknesses.mechanism: No upper bound or row-count validation — GPT adds phantom rows (Mammafiore Olio)

weaknesses.text: If quantity truly cannot be determined: quantity = null
weaknesses.mechanism: Rarely used — GPT prefers guessing over null

weaknesses.text: quantity, unit_price, and total are each authoritative — copy each exactly
weaknesses.mechanism: Contradicts infer-from-name rule; GPT still misreads columns (Prosciutto 8.17→17)

weaknesses.text: quantity × unit_price may NOT equal total. Never alter to force closure.
weaknesses.mechanism: Allows inconsistent triples — Ginger Beer 24×€0.85=€19.38 passes despite wrong semantics
No instruction to reject lot numbers, footer rows, or sub-lines as separate items
